seo.title: E-Commerce Code Generation AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for code generation AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents that generate and execute code in e-commerce environments introduces compounding risks: a single hallucinated pricing function or misconfigured checkout snippet can trigger FTC enforcement, PCI DSS violations, and mass customer refund disputes simultaneously. Code generation agents must be held to the same compliance standards as human developers, with additional guardrails for autonomous execution, dependency injection, and real-time data handling. This checklist ensures your AI code generation pipeline meets e-commerce regulatory requirements while protecting revenue, customer trust, and brand integrity.

sections.heading: Code Safety & Sandbox Execution Controls
sections.description: Ensure all AI-generated code is validated, sandboxed, and audited before touching production e-commerce systems, payment flows, or customer data pipelines.

items.label: Enforce Isolated Sandbox Execution
items.description: All AI-generated code must execute in a network-isolated sandbox (e.g., Docker container with no outbound internet access) before any promotion to staging or production. Verify sandbox cannot reach payment processors, customer databases, or third-party APIs.

items.label: Scan for Hardcoded Credentials and API Keys
items.description: Run automated secret-detection tools (e.g., Trufflehog, GitLeaks) on every code artifact the agent produces before execution. Reject and re-prompt any output containing API keys, payment tokens, database passwords, or OAuth secrets.

items.label: Validate Dependency Manifest Against Approved Allowlist
items.description: Cross-reference every npm, pip, or composer package the agent introduces against a pre-approved dependency allowlist. Flag any package with known CVEs (CVSS score ≥ 7.0) or packages not previously vetted by your security team.

items.label: Enforce Syntax and Static Analysis Gates
items.description: Run language-specific linters (ESLint for JavaScript, Pylint for Python) and static analysis tools (Semgrep, Bandit) on all generated code. Block execution if any critical-severity finding is returned, including SQL injection patterns or eval() usage.

items.label: Limit Sandbox Resource Quotas
items.description: Cap CPU, memory, and execution time for sandbox runs (e.g., 2 CPU cores, 512MB RAM, 30-second timeout) to prevent runaway loops or resource exhaustion attacks from malformed generated code.

items.label: Log All Generated Code Artifacts with Immutable Audit Trail
items.description: Store every code artifact the agent generates, including failed attempts and iterative refinements, in an append-only audit log with timestamps, agent session IDs, and the triggering prompt. Retain logs for minimum 12 months for compliance review.

items.label: Require Human Approval Gate for Payment-Adjacent Code
items.description: Any generated code that touches checkout flows, pricing calculations, discount logic, or payment form rendering must require explicit human engineer sign-off before deployment, regardless of automated test pass rates.

sections.heading: E-Commerce Pricing & Product Claim Accuracy
sections.description: AI-generated code that renders prices, discounts, or product descriptions must be validated against live data sources to prevent FTC violations and customer-facing pricing errors.

items.label: Validate Pricing Logic Against Live Catalog API
items.description: After code generation, run automated integration tests that call your live product catalog API and assert that rendered prices match source-of-truth values within a 0% tolerance. Any discrepancy must block deployment.

items.label: Test Discount and Promotion Calculation Functions
items.description: Generate a test suite covering edge cases: stacked discounts, expired coupon codes, currency rounding, and tax-inclusive vs. tax-exclusive pricing. AI-generated discount logic must pass all edge case assertions before promotion.

items.label: Prevent AI from Generating Unsubstantiated Product Claim Copy
items.description: If the agent generates marketing copy or product description strings, route output through a claims-validation prompt that checks for superlatives ('best,' 'guaranteed,' 'clinically proven') and flags them for human substantiation review per FTC guidelines.

items.label: Enforce Currency and Locale Formatting Correctness
items.description: Test all generated price-rendering functions against a matrix of locales (en-US, en-GB, de-DE, fr-FR) to ensure correct currency symbols, decimal separators, and thousands separators. Incorrect formatting can constitute misleading pricing under GDPR-adjacent consumer protection laws.

items.label: Assert Return and Refund Policy Strings Are Not Modified
items.description: If generated code renders return/refund policy text, compare output strings against the canonical policy document using exact-match or semantic similarity checks. Any deviation must trigger a compliance review before deployment.

items.label: Validate Inventory Availability Logic for Accuracy
items.description: Test generated inventory-check functions against mock datasets that include out-of-stock, backorder, and limited-quantity scenarios. Ensure the code never displays 'In Stock' for unavailable items, which can trigger FTC deceptive advertising claims.

items.label: Audit AI-Generated A/B Test Logic for Price Discrimination
items.description: Review any generated experimentation or personalization code to ensure pricing variations do not constitute illegal price discrimination based on protected characteristics. Document all pricing experiment parameters for FTC audit readiness.

sections.heading: PCI DSS Compliance for Payment Code
sections.description: AI-generated code must never introduce vulnerabilities into payment flows. Strict controls are required to maintain PCI DSS scope boundaries and protect cardholder data.

items.label: Prohibit Agent from Generating Code That Handles Raw Card Data
items.description: Configure the agent's system prompt and output filters to refuse generation of any code that accepts, stores, logs, or transmits raw PAN (Primary Account Numbers), CVV, or expiration dates. All payment tokenization must route through your certified payment processor SDK.

items.label: Validate That Generated Checkout Code Uses Approved Payment SDK Versions
items.description: Automatically check that any payment SDK imports (Stripe.js, Braintree, Square) in generated code reference versions on your approved list and are not outdated versions with known PCI DSS compliance gaps.

items.label: Scan Generated Code for Logging of Sensitive Payment Fields
items.description: Run regex and AST-based scans on generated code to detect any console.log, print, or logger statements that could capture payment form field values, order totals with card references, or authentication tokens.

items.label: Enforce HTTPS-Only Assertions in Generated Network Code
items.description: Validate that all generated fetch(), axios, or HTTP client calls targeting payment or checkout endpoints use HTTPS with certificate validation enabled. Reject any generated code using HTTP or disabling SSL verification.

items.label: Test Generated Code Against OWASP Top 10 E-Commerce Attack Patterns
items.description: Run generated checkout and cart code through automated DAST scans targeting XSS, CSRF, and injection vulnerabilities specific to e-commerce flows before any staging deployment.

items.label: Ensure Generated Code Does Not Expand PCI DSS Scope
items.description: Have a PCI-qualified security assessor review any AI-generated code that touches the checkout page, order confirmation, or payment webhook handlers to confirm it does not introduce new systems into PCI DSS scope.

items.label: Validate 3DS2 and SCA Implementation Correctness
items.description: If the agent generates Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) or 3D Secure 2.0 flows, run integration tests against your payment processor's sandbox environment to confirm correct challenge/frictionless flow handling and compliance with PSD2 requirements.

sections.heading: Privacy Compliance & ADA Accessibility in Generated Code
sections.description: AI-generated frontend and backend code must meet CCPA/GDPR data handling requirements and ADA/WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards to avoid regulatory penalties and exclusionary user experiences.

items.label: Scan Generated Code for Unauthorized Customer Data Collection
items.description: Analyze all generated JavaScript and backend code for undisclosed data collection patterns: hidden form fields capturing PII, third-party pixel injections, or localStorage writes containing customer identifiers not covered by your privacy policy.

items.label: Validate Consent Gate Logic Before Any Data Processing Code Executes
items.description: If the agent generates analytics, personalization, or marketing code, assert that it checks for valid CCPA opt-out or GDPR consent status before executing. Test with mock consent states: opted-out, consented, and no-preference.

items.label: Enforce Data Minimization in Generated API Calls
items.description: Review all generated API request payloads to ensure they do not transmit customer PII fields beyond what is strictly necessary for the function. Flag any generated code sending full customer profiles to third-party services when only an order ID is required.

items.label: Run WCAG 2.1 AA Automated Accessibility Checks on Generated UI Code
items.description: Pass all generated HTML, JSX, and CSS through axe-core or Lighthouse accessibility audits. Require zero critical violations (missing alt text, insufficient color contrast, missing form labels, keyboard trap patterns) before deployment.

items.label: Validate ARIA Roles and Semantic HTML in Generated Checkout Components
items.description: Specifically audit AI-generated checkout form components for correct ARIA labeling on payment fields, error message associations (aria-describedby), and focus management during multi-step checkout flows to meet ADA compliance requirements.

items.label: Assert Data Retention Limits Are Enforced in Generated Database Code
items.description: Review any generated database schema migrations or query code to ensure customer PII fields include retention metadata and that generated cleanup jobs align with your CCPA/GDPR data retention policy (typically 12-24 months post-transaction).

items.label: Verify Right-to-Deletion Logic Is Not Broken by Generated Code
items.description: After deploying AI-generated data model changes, run end-to-end tests of your CCPA/GDPR deletion request workflow to confirm the new code does not orphan customer records or break cascade deletion across order history, reviews, and profile data.

items.label: Document All Third-Party Services Introduced by Generated Code for Privacy Policy Updates
items.description: Maintain an automated inventory of all third-party SDK imports and API endpoints in AI-generated code. Trigger a privacy policy review workflow whenever a new third-party data processor is introduced that is not already disclosed to customers.

notes.note: AI-generated code that dynamically renders product descriptions, customer reviews, or promotional banners must not fabricate or exaggerate product claims. Any AI-generated marketing copy strings must be reviewed against FTC substantiation standards before being hardcoded or served via CMS. Automated price comparison features generated by the agent must reflect genuine market prices, not manipulated reference prices, to avoid FTC 'fake markdown' enforcement actions.

AI-generated data pipeline code must implement privacy-by-design: no customer PII should be processed without a lawful basis, and all generated ETL or analytics scripts must be reviewed to confirm they respect opt-out signals (CCPA Global Privacy Control) and consent records (GDPR). Generated code that introduces new data flows must trigger a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) review before production deployment.

PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 6.2 mandates that all bespoke and custom software, including AI-generated code, is developed according to secure coding practices. Requirement 6.3 requires an inventory of all bespoke software and protection from known vulnerabilities. AI-generated payment-adjacent code must be included in your quarterly vulnerability scanning program and annual penetration testing scope to maintain PCI DSS certification.

notes.regulation: ADA Title III and WCAG 2.1 AA (28 CFR Part 36)
notes.note: Federal courts have consistently ruled that e-commerce websites are places of public accommodation under ADA Title III. AI-generated frontend code must meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards at minimum. Automated accessibility testing alone is insufficient; generated interactive components (modals, carousels, dynamic cart updates) must also undergo manual screen reader testing with NVDA and VoiceOver before deployment to avoid ADA litigation exposure.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI code generation pipeline against PCI DSS, FTC, CCPA, and ADA requirements—catching compliance gaps before they reach production and protecting your revenue, customers, and brand.
